findings: The unbounded path was watchdog -> agentic review / verifier remediation, which could sit in the ACPX-based agentic review until killed.
The watchdog now forces a bounded watchdog mode for its own agentic/verifier calls and explicitly skips expensive agentic review in that path.
Watchdog-path failures remain honest: the skipped agentic review writes a fail artifact, keeps loopHealthy=false, and verifier/watchdog exit non-zero on broken docs.
repairsApplied: Added watchdog-mode env flags to docs_stack_temp_watchdog.py and passed them into direct agentic review plus remediation/verifier invocations.
Updated ralph_docs_agentic_review.py to honor watchdog flags: either fast-skip agentic review with explicit failure artifacts or use a shorter timeout when watchdog mode is set.
Updated ralph_docs_verify.py to propagate watchdog-mode env flags into its child script executions.
proof: py_compile passed for docs_stack_temp_watchdog.py, ralph_docs_agentic_review.py, and ralph_docs_verify.py.
Forced watchdog run completed on its own with EXIT=1 in 4.12s while docs were still broken.
Direct verifier run under watchdog flags completed on its own with EXIT=1 in 1.65s and recorded the explicit bounded-skip failure artifact.
